texto: And when the firemen turned off the hose and were standing in the wet, smoky room, Jim's Aunt, Miss. Prothero, came downstairs and peered in at them. texto: Go on thinking that you don't need to be read and you'll find that it may become quite true: no one will feel the need tom read it because it is written for yourself alone and the public won't feel any impulse to gate crash such a private party.
explicacao: Citação de Dylan Thomas sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Go on thinking that you don't need to be read and you'll find that it may become quite true: no o…” articula reflexao, thinking, yourself em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como yourself e party.

frase_original: Go on thinking that you don't need to be read and you'll find that it may become quite true: no one will feel the need tom read it because it is written for yourself alone and the public won't feel any impulse to gate crash such a private party.

texto: In my craft or sullen artExercised in the still nightWhen only the moon ragesAnd the lovers lie abedWith all their griefs in their arms,I labour by singing lightNot for ambition or breadOr the strut and trade of charmsOn the ivory stagesBut for the common wagesOf their most secret heart.Not for the proud man apartFrom the raging moon I writeOn these spindrift pagesNor for the towering deadWith their nightingales and psalmsBut for the lovers, their armsRound the griefs of the ages,Who pay no praise or wagesNor heed my craft or art.
explicacao: Citação de Dylan Thomas sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “In my craft or sullen artExercised in the still nightWhen only the moon ragesAnd the lovers lie a…” articula amor, writing, reflexao em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e luto.
